title: How to remove the dashed line at the bottom of automated emails?
excerpt:
On all my automated emails there is a line at the bottom like this: ------------ 
How do I get rid of this?

posts.author: ccosta (Courtney Costa)
posts.cookedHtml:
On all my automated emails there is a line at the bottom like this: ------------

How do I get rid of this?

posts.author: JaredJLY (Jared Young)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also needing to do this, does anyone know how?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Courtney Costa:

On all my automated emails there is a line at the bottom like this: ------------

Hey, I checked all my email notifcations and this didnt appear. Do you have an example? I wonder if it is the template? In Monday.com, the presence of an extra line at the bottom of automated emails is likely caused by either the formatting of the email body in the automation or a placeholder/default text included by the system.

Here’s how you can resolve or investigate this issue:

  1. Check the Automation Template:
  • Go to the automation generating the email.
  • Review the email content, including custom fields and any text formatting. Make sure there are no unnecessary line breaks or placeholders at the end of the email body.

posts.author: JaredJLY (Jared Young)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Clare,

I have linked 3 different emails and recreated the automation trying different texts but I still get -------- at the very bottom of the email above the signature. Is there anything else I can look at?

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Coud you show me a screenshot of an example I tried this but didnt get lines. I wonder if the email service makes a differance?

image907×130 8.77 KB

posts.author: harrycmary (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml: To remove the dashed line at the bottom of automated emails, check the email template or signature settings in your email automation platform. The dashed line is often part of the default styling for separating the footer or signature. Editing the template’s HTML or disabling the footer separator should resolve the issue. If you’re unsure where to find this setting, consult the documentation of your email tool.
